title: AI Automation Engineer
company_focus: Human transformation and professional coaching platform
description:
Let’s face it, a company whose mission is human
transformation better have some fresh thinking about the
employer/employee relationship.

We do. We can’t cram it all in here, but you’ll start
noticing it from the first interview.

Even our candidate experience is different. And when you get
an offer from us (and accept it), you get way more than a
paycheck. You get a personal BetterUp Coach, a development
plan, a trained and coached manager, the most amazing team
you’ve ever met (yes, each with their own personal BetterUp
Coach), and most importantly, work that matters.

This makes for a remarkably focused and fulfilling work
experience. Frankly, it’s not for everyone. But for people
with fire in their belly, it’s a game-changing, career-
defining, soul-lifting move.

Join us and we promise you the most intense and fulfilling
years of your career, doing life-changing work in a fun,
inventive, soulful culture.

If that sounds exciting—and the job description below feels
like a fit—we really should start talking.

We are a hybrid company with a focus on in-person
collaboration when necessary. Employees are expected to be
available to work from one of our office hubs at least two
days per week, or eight days per month. Our US hub locations
include: Austin, TX; Chicago, IL; New York City, NY; San
Francisco, CA; and the Washington, DC metro area. If this is
a role based in Europe, our Europe hub locations are London,
UK and Amsterdam, NL. Please ensure you can realistically
commit to this structure before applying.

We’re looking for an AI Automation and Integration Engineer
to join our rapidly evolving IT & AI Workspace Technology
team. This role is for a hands-on builder who thrives on
creating smarter, more connected systems—someone who can
transform manual workflows into intelligent, AI-enabled
automations using cutting-edge tools like Model Context
Protocol (MCP), Slack workflows, and secure system
integrations.

You’ll design and implement scalable automations across our
SaaS ecosystem, partner closely with our AI product and
infrastructure teams, and bring a high degree of technical
rigor and curiosity to every problem you solve.

This is a key role on a small, fast-moving team where your
work will directly impact how we scale, automate, and
future-proof our internal operations.

What you’ll do:

MCP & AI Workflow Engineering

- Implement and scale our Model Context Protocol (MCP) to
  support secure, composable AI workflows across Slack,
  Jira, Salesforce, GitHub, AWS-based infrastructure, LLM
  providers (e.g., Claude), and internal APIs.
- Design agentic systems that enable AI tooling to integrate
  seamlessly with operational data and user identity
  services.
- Author requests for comments (RFCs), technical requirements
  documents (TRDs), and internal documentation to align MCP
  features with enterprise use cases.

Automation & Integration Development

- Build and maintain scalable automations and secure APIs to
  reduce operational overhead in onboarding, provisioning,
  and data lifecycle management.
- Own development and maintenance of reusable modules for
  identity sync, data uploads (e.g., CSV bulk importers),
  and AI-to-API orchestration.
- Drive continuous improvement across automation platforms by
  collaborating with stakeholder teams across the business.

Security, Identity & System Governance

- Work with InfoSec and IT teams to enforce role-based access
  controls and SAML/SSO integrations
- Harden identity workflows across US and EU regions, ensuring
  compliance with internal and external standards.
- Support audit readiness through traceable change management
  and data integrity practices.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

- Attend cross functional standups, Architecture Roundtables,
  and AI working groups to ensure engineering solutions
  align with company goals.
- Partner closely with stakeholders to balance speed and
  safety in fast-moving pilot initiatives.
- Advocate for test-driven development (TDD), documentation,
  and open source-inspired code reusability.

What Success Looks Like

- MCP server scaled and adopted across multiple workflows with
  stable, secure integrations
- Automation measurably reduced manual data entry through bulk
  import and automation tooling (e.g., 50% reduction).
- Onboarding and provisioning latency improved through
  identity automation (e.g., 30% faster completion).
- Clear documentation, playbooks, and dashboards enable
  stakeholders to self-serve with AI tools and automation
  templates, not rely on ad-hoc IT custom work.
- All automation systems have strong test coverage, robust
  monitoring, and meet security review requirements, with
  audit-ready logs for provisioning and data movement.
- Impact is visible and quantified through simple time-saved
  and adoption metrics layered on top of Jira, dashboards,
  and usage telemetry.

If you have some or all of the following, please apply:

Required:

- 3–6 years of professional experience in software or systems
  engineering, with demonstrated ability to own backend
  services, integrations, or automation workflows with
  minimal oversight.
- Experience integrating AI capabilities into production
  systems, including orchestrating LLM-powered workflows
  across APIs, event-driven systems, and internal services.
- Hands-on experience designing AI-assisted automations that
  operate on real business data with appropriate context,
  permissions, and safeguards.
- Familiarity with using LLM providers (e.g., Claude or
  similar) as part of larger systems rather than standalone
  chat or prompt-based tools.
- Demonstrated experience integrating APIs and building
  automations across complex SaaS ecosystems.
- Proficiency in n8n and Zapier or custom-built orchestration
  tools
- Hands-on experience building pipelines or automations using
  MCP, Step Functions, or equivalent event-driven
  architectures
- Strong scripting or development skills (e.g., Python, ROR or
  JavaScript) for writing custom connectors or data
  transformations
- Familiarity with common SaaS platforms including Okta, Jira,
  Slack, Workday, Google Workspace, FreshService, and their
  APIs
- Experience working with authentication protocols (e.g.,
  SAML, OAuth), and systems like Okta, GCP, or AWS.

Preferred:

- Experience operating MCP-based or similar AI-enabled
  workflows in production environments, including
  considerations for reliability, observability, and ongoing
  maintenance.
- Prior contributions to audit, compliance, or governance-
  related tooling, such as provisioning logs, access
  tracking, or data integrity checks.
- Strong documentation practices, including maintaining
  technical artifacts (RFCs, diagrams, runbooks, and
  playbooks) as part of day-to-day work.
- Comfort acting as an embedded technical partner to non-
  technical stakeholders (e.g., HR, Recruiting, People Ops,
  Finance), with the ability to explain complex systems
  clearly, set expectations, and co-own automation roadmaps.
- Familiarity with operating and debugging production systems
  using tools such as logging, monitoring, and basic data
  stores (e.g., DataDog, Postgres, Redis), as well as
  supporting file-based or “last mile” integrations when
  needed.
- Comfort with working as an embedded partner with non-
  technical stakeholders (EAs, HR, Recruiting, People Ops,
  Finance), including training them on automation patterns
  and co-owning roadmaps for the next set of high-impact
  automations.

AI at BetterUp

Our team thrives at the intersection of human expertise and
AI capability. As an AI-forward company, adaptation and
continuous learning are part of our daily work. We're
looking for teammates who are excited to evolve alongside
technology – people who experiment boldly, share their
discoveries openly, and help define best practices for AI-
augmented work. These professionals thoughtfully integrate
AI into their work to deliver exceptional results while
maintaining the human judgment and creativity that drives
real innovation. During our interview process, you’ll have
opportunities to showcase how you harness AI to learn,
iterate, and amplify your impact.

Benefits:

At BetterUp, we are committed to living out our mission
every day and that starts with providing benefits that allow
our employees to care for themselves, support their
families, and give back to their community.

- Access to BetterUp coaching; one for you and one for a
  friend or family member
- A competitive compensation plan with opportunity for
  advancement
- Medical, dental, and vision insurance
- Flexible paid time off
- Per year:
  - All federal/statutory holidays observed
  - 4 BetterUp Inner Workdays (https://www.betterup.co/inner-
    work)
  - 5 Volunteer Days to give back
  - Learning and Development stipend
  - Company wide Summer & Winter breaks
- Year-round charitable contribution of your choice on behalf
  of BetterUp
- 401(k) self contribution

We are dedicated to building diverse teams that fuel an
authentic workplace and sense of belonging for each and
every employee. We know applying for a job can be
intimidating, please don’t hesitate to reach out — we
encourage everyone interested in joining us to apply.

BetterUp Inc. provides equal employment opportunities (EEO)
to all employees and applicants for employment without
regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin,
disability, genetics, gender, sexual orientation, age,
marital status, veteran status. In addition to federal law
requirements, BetterUp Inc. complies with applicable state
and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in
every location in which the company has facilities. This
policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment,
including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion,
termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence,
compensation, and training.

At BetterUp, we compensate our employees fairly for their
work. Base salary is determined by job-related experience,
education/training, residence location, as well as market
indicators. The range below is representative of base salary
only and does not include equity, sales bonus plans (when
applicable) and benefits. This range may be modified in the
future.

The base salary range for the role is $144,000 to $227,000.

Protecting your privacy and treating your personal
information with care is very important to us, and central
to the entire BetterUp family. By submitting your
application, you acknowledge that your personal information
will be processed in accordance with our Applicant Privacy
Notice. If you have any questions about the privacy of your
personal information or your rights with regards to your
personal information, please reach out to
support@betterup.co

#LI-Hybrid

Design agentic systems that enable AI tooling to integrate seamlessly with operational data and user identity services
Build and maintain scalable automations and secure APIs to reduce operational overhead in onboarding, provisioning, and data lifecycle management
Own development and maintenance of reusable modules for identity sync, data uploads, and AI-to-API orchestration
Work with InfoSec and IT teams to enforce role-based access controls and SAML/SSO integrations
Harden identity workflows across US and EU regions, ensuring compliance with internal and external standards
Partner closely with stakeholders to balance speed and safety in fast-moving pilot initiatives

use_cases: Automated internal operations and workflow optimization across SaaS ecosystem
Secure AI-enabled employee onboarding and provisioning systems
Identity and access management automation with SAML/SSO integration

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves hands-on implementation of AI systems
including Model Context Protocol (MCP), agentic systems, and
LLM-powered workflows. The engineer directly builds and
deploys AI-enabled automations and integrates LLM providers
(Claude) into production systems.
